sast_review.reason: The regex `^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$` is applied to user-controlled email input. While anchored, the `+` quantifiers on `[^@]` can lead to polynomial backtracking on crafted strings (e.g., many periods or repeated patterns). No length validation exists. Exploitability is limited by single-email-per-request and HTTP size limits, but the finding is not clearly a false positive.

raw.summary: The SAST finding (CWE-1333 polynomial ReDoS) is valid. The regex `^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$` on line 41 uses `+` quantifiers on character classes matching any non-`@` character, applied to user-controlled email input. Although the regex is anchored with `^` and `$`, it can still exhibit polynomial backtracking on crafted inputs (e.g., long strings of periods or repeated patterns). No input length limit is enforced. The finding is marked UNCERTAIN because exploitability is moderate (single email per request, bounded by HTTP size), but it cannot be dismissed as FP without additional safeguards. Functionality requirements are fully met.
